palette_hint: Deep charcoal background #1a1814, warm off-white text #f0ebe1, with burnished gold accent #c9a84c and muted terracotta #a05c3b for secondary highlights.
type_style: Headings in Georgia (serif) at weights 400 and 700 with wide letter-spacing; body in system-ui at weight 300 and 400; a deliberate modular scale from 0.875rem to 4.5rem creating a stately, old-world editorial feel.
description: Maison Cendrée is a French-inflected fine dining restaurant in a converted 19th-century mill in Asheville, NC, serving a nightly six-course tasting menu built around wood-fire cookery and Appalachian ingredients.
constraints: Maximum content column width of 780px, centered, with horizontal page padding of 48px on desktop and 24px on mobile.
Typographic scale must use exactly: 0.875rem (captions/labels), 1rem (body), 1.5rem (subheadings), 2.75rem (section headings), 4.5rem (hero display text).
All section headings use letter-spacing: 0.18em and font-weight: 400; hero display text uses letter-spacing: 0.08em and font-weight: 700.

Body copy uses line-height: 1.8; headlines use line-height: 1.1; pull quotes use font-size 1.5rem, font-style italic, line-height 1.5, color #c9a84c, with a 2px left border in #c9a84c and 24px left padding.
Drop caps on the opening paragraph of the Philosophy and Cellar pages: ::first-letter sized at 4.5rem, float left, font-weight 700, color #c9a84c, line-height 0.85, margin-right 8px.
Horizontal rules between sections use a 1px border in #c9a84c at 40% opacity, with 48px vertical margin above and below.
No decorative images; all visual interest comes from typography, spacing, and color alone.

page_specs.philosophy: A single long-form essay page opening with a large 4.5rem display heading 'Fire, Grain & Memory' followed by a drop-cap opening paragraph describing chef Mireille Aubert's childhood in Lyon and her move to Appalachia. Includes two pull quotes: one on sourcing heritage grains from Cairn Springs Mill and one on the restaurant's wood-fire hearth built from reclaimed millstones. Ends with a short manifesto list of the restaurant's five guiding principles in 0.875rem spaced uppercase labels.
page_specs.menu: Presents the current six-course tasting menu titled 'Autumn Progression — October 2024' with each course as a labeled section: Amuse, First, Second, Fish, Main, and Dessert. Each course entry uses a 1.5rem italic dish name in Georgia, a 1rem ingredient list in off-white, and a 0.875rem caption in terracotta #a05c3b noting the sourcing farm or producer. A short note at the bottom states the menu is $145 per person with optional beverage pairing at $85, with dietary accommodations available 72 hours in advance.
page_specs.cellar: A narrative page about the restaurant's wine and spirits program curated by sommelier Théo Vaillant, opening with a drop cap. Organized into three sections — 'The Loire Obsession,' 'Appalachian Spirits,' and 'Zero-Proof Pairings' — each introduced with a 2.75rem section heading. Contains one pull quote from Théo: 'A great Muscadet doesn't ask to be noticed — it asks to belong.' Closes with a note that the cellar holds approximately 280 labels and private selections are available for table-side consultation.
page_specs.reservations: A reservations information page (no interactive form) with a 4.5rem hero heading 'Reserve Your Evening' followed by structured text blocks covering service times (Wednesday–Sunday, single seating at 7:00 PM), party size limits (2–8 guests), deposit policy ($50 per person, credited to the final bill), and cancellation terms (48-hour notice required). Includes a clearly labeled section for private dining inquiries with the contact email events@maisoncendree.com in gold #c9a84c. Ends with a pull quote: 'We set aside sixty seats each evening — each one matters to us.'
page_specs.find-us: A spare, typographically rich page giving location and arrival details for the mill building at 412 Riverside Mill Road, Asheville, NC 28801. Includes sections for driving directions from downtown Asheville (12 minutes via Merrimon Ave), valet parking details ($15, included on Fridays), and a note about the unmarked gravel entrance off Riverside Road marked only by a single cast-iron lantern. Lists the phone number (828) 554-0917 and hours for the host desk (Tuesday–Sunday, 2:00 PM–10:00 PM). All address and directional text is set in 0.875rem uppercase with 0.18em letter-spacing in the terracotta accent color.
